title: INTERNAL Error: Attempted to access index 3 within vector of size 3
description:
### What happens?

Running this query:
```
select * from 'SecuritiesOrganizationsMapping__UPDATES.parquet' limit 10;
```
Produces:
```
INTERNAL Error: Attempted to access index 3 within vector of size 3
This error signals an assertion failure within DuckDB. This usually occurs due to unexpected conditions or errors in the program's logic.
For more information, see https://duckdb.org/docs/dev/internal_errors
```
I'm able to see the file with the "Parquet Floor v5.0.1" utility.
[SecuritiesOrganizationsMapping_Bulk__UPDATES.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17619409/SecuritiesOrganizationsMapping_Bulk__UPDATES.zip)


### To Reproduce

query the attached file

### OS:

x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.2

### DuckDB Client:

cli

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Nestor Sulikowski

### Affiliation:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value, created_at) VALUES (1, 'A', 10.5, TIMESTAMP('2023-01-01 10:00:00'));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value, created_at) VALUES (2, 'B', NULL, TIMESTAMP('2023-01-02 11:00:00'));
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL OR value > 5.0;
SELECT name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count FROM test_table LIMIT 5;
key_bug_features: Accessing index out of bounds in vector
